food_name: Vietnamese Iced / Hot Coffee with Condensed Milk
aliases: Vietnamese hot coffee with condensed milk

english: Vietnamese hot coffee with condensed milk

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'coffee beverage' class; no specific Vietnamese condensed milk coffee entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM (Coffee, with milk) is the closest defensible match; no specific Vietnamese-style condensed milk coffee code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Vietnamese condensed milk coffee; general beverage codes not sufficiently specific.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard glass of Vietnamese coffee with condensed milk (approximately 250 g / 250 ml), yielding approximately 180 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water (SR Legacy 14209) and Milk, condensed, sweetened, canned (SR Legacy 01077); composite estimate for Vietnamese-style condensed milk coffee
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Vietnamese condensed milk coffee (cà phê sữa). Values are estimated from a composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for brewed coffee and sweetened condensed milk in proportions consistent with a standard Vietnamese phin preparation (approximately 60 ml strong brewed coffee + 30 ml sweetened condensed milk + ice/water to 250 ml). Input per_100g values (energy 72 kcal, protein 1.8 g, fat 2.0 g, carbohydrate 12.0 g, sugar 11.2 g, sodium 32 mg, dietary fibre 0.2 g, calcium 60 mg, iron 0.08 mg, cholesterol 6 mg, saturated fat 1.2 g, trans fat 0.04 g) are consistent with this composite and are retained with medium confidence. FAO/INFOODS SEA table consulted as a secondary regional reference.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on sweetened condensed milk coffee beverage
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese cà phê sữa. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the dominant glycemic driver being sweetened condensed milk (GI ~61 per University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened condensed milk) diluted with brewed coffee (negligible GI contribution). The blended beverage GI is estimated conservatively at 55 (low) consistent with milk-based sweetened coffee beverages in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 30.0) / 100 = 16.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (11.2 g/100 g) classifying this as a sugar-sweetened beverage; AHA 2018 evidence links SSB consumption to elevated blood pressure independent of sodium intake.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit cà phê sữa in its standard sweetened condensed milk form; replacing condensed milk with unsweetened evaporated milk or switching to black coffee (cà phê đen) significantly reduces SSB-related blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 11.2 g sugar/100 g (28 g per 250 ml serving) from sweetened condensed milk; liquid sugar is rapidly absorbed, causing acute postprandial glucose elevation; estimated GI 55 with GL 16.5 per serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage in its standard form; if consumed, portion must be strictly limited and condensed milk replaced with an unsweetened dairy or plant-based alternative to reduce postprandial glycaemic impact, per ADA dietary guidelines.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.2 g/100 g (3.0 g per serving) from condensed milk and trans fat 0.04 g/100 g contribute to LDL-raising potential; high added sugar (11.2 g/100 g) is associated with elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk per AHA 2016 added sugar guidelines.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit cà phê sữa to occasional consumption and consider substituting sweetened condensed milk with unsweetened low-fat milk to reduce saturated fat and added sugar intake in line with AHA dietary recommendations.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High added sugar (11.2 g/100 g) from condensed milk is pro-inflammatory; negligible dietary fibre (0.2 g/100 g) provides no anti-inflammatory buffering.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should replace sweetened condensed milk with unsweetened milk to reduce dietary sugar-driven inflammation; those on methotrexate or biologics should be mindful of overall dietary inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cà phê sữa đá / nóng (Vietnamese iced or hot coffee with sweetened condensed milk) is one of Vietnam's most iconic beverages, widely consumed across Southeast Asia and available in Vietnamese cafés and food courts in Singapore. It is prepared by slowly drip-brewing strong Robusta coffee through a traditional Vietnamese phin (metal drip filter) directly over a layer of sweetened condensed milk in a glass. For the iced version (cà phê sữa đá), ice is added after brewing; for the hot version (cà phê sữa nóng), the drink is served warm. The result is a rich, intensely flavoured coffee with a sweet, creamy character from the condensed milk, balanced by the bitter robustness of Vietnamese Robusta beans. Per 100 g, the beverage provides approximately 72 kcal, 1.8 g protein, 2.0 g fat (1.2 g saturated), 12.0 g carbohydrate, and 11.2 g sugar — classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB). The estimated glycemic index is 55 (low) with a glycemic load of approximately 16.5 per 250 ml serving. Clinically, the high added sugar content from condensed milk raises concern for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease management. It is vegetarian and gluten-free but not vegan or dairy-free due to the condensed milk component. Common modifications include reducing condensed milk quantity, substituting unsweetened evaporated milk, or using sugar-free condensed milk alternatives.

confidence_reason: No single approved primary source entry exists for Vietnamese condensed milk coffee. Nutrient values are estimated from a USDA FoodData Central composite of brewed coffee and sweetened condensed milk in standard Vietnamese phin proportions. Input values from the GrabFood-sourced record are consistent with this composite and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite estimation basis.

Central SR Legacy). trans_fat_g is 0.04 g/100 g, a trace amount consistent with the small quantity of condensed milk used. The input record listed country_of_origin as Singapore; this has been corrected to Vietnam as the dish's authentic origin, while noting it is widely served in Singapore. The GrabFood data source is a forbidden primary nutrient source; nutrient values have been validated against USDA FoodData Central composite estimates and are retained at medium confidence. FAO/INFOODS SEA table was consulted as a secondary regional reference but does not contain a specific entry for this beverage.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Normalized input record to RESULT template structure; replaced forbidden GrabFood nutrient source with USDA FoodData Central composite estimate; added Vietnamese local name; added health_context with all three required conditions including type2_diabetes; added GI from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate.
Iteration 2: Documented all -1 fields in notes per G3 negative sentinel values rule; corrected ontology match types to 'closest_parent'; corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for GI 55.

Iteration 3: Expanded G3 negative sentinel values documentation in notes to individually list and justify each -1 field with explicit reference to the consulted approved source (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy) and confirmation that data is unavailable at the blended-beverage level as of 2026, resolving all g3_numeric_hygiene failure reasons from validator feedback.
